name: Xiaohongshu MCP
what_it_does: An MCP server that enables AI agents to publish content, search posts, retrieve recommendations, manage comments, and access user profiles on Xiaohongshu (小红书/RedNote) via headless browser automation.
use_cases: Automated content distribution and cross-posting to Xiaohongshu from AI workflows
Social media management bots that schedule and publish image or video posts
Analytics collection: scraping engagement metrics, follower counts, and post performance
Batch comment management and audience engagement automation
not_for: High-volume publishing (recommended max 50 posts/day to avoid detection)
Multi-account simultaneous management (single web client per account)
Users outside China or without verified Xiaohongshu accounts
best_when: You need an AI agent to autonomously publish and manage content on Xiaohongshu as part of a broader social media workflow.
avoid_when: You need to manage multiple accounts simultaneously, require high posting volume, or operate in environments where Xiaohongshu account verification is difficult.

auth.notes: Requires manual browser login on first use to establish session cookies. Sessions persist locally. Only one web client per account simultaneously.

pricing.notes: Open source. Requires running your own Docker/Go server.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Headless browser automation errors are hard to surface cleanly to MCP callers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Publishing posts is not idempotent by nature; no deduplication documented.
known_agent_gotchas: Manual browser login required on first use — cannot be fully automated
Single session per account; concurrent agent use will break the session
Xiaohongshu platform changes can break headless browser automation without warning
Recommended 50 posts/day max — agents must enforce rate limits themselves
Post title limit of 20 characters and body limit of 1000 characters must be respected
Platform ToS may prohibit automated posting; account ban risk exists

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
